decoded.explanation: The defendant abused their position of trust as an accountant to embezzle $25,000 from their employer. While embezzlement is a serious breach of fiduciary duty, several mitigating factors warrant a sentence at the lower end of the advisory range. The defendant has no prior criminal record, demonstrating a history of law-abiding behavior, and has made active efforts to rectify the harm by partially paying back the stolen funds. A sentence of 4 months of incarceration, followed by supervised release with the condition of full restitution, sufficiently addresses the seriousness of the offense, provides just punishment, and deters future conduct while acknowledging the defendant's mitigating circumstances.
